summary: Backend-only smoke pass for Outstanding Documents Portal. Auth (admin login + cookie + Bearer + /auth/me + logout), client CRUD (after _id workaround), CSV upload (3 rows, re-upload replaces), client outstanding endpoints, submission validation (400s), role enforcement (403s), admin submissions listing/filter, admin reset item, SMTP settings persist & password not echoed — all working. Two HIGH bugs discovered: ID serialization (`_id` instead of `id`) and submission timeout/502 when SMTP host unreachable.
passed: POST /api/auth/login returns user + access_token and sets access_token cookie
GET /api/auth/me works with both cookie session AND Authorization: Bearer header
GET /api/admin/clients lists clients with purchase_outstanding & sales_outstanding counts
GET /api/admin/clients?q=<term> case-insensitive search by business_name works
GET /api/admin/clients/{id}, PUT update, DELETE all work (when using _id from create response)
Re-uploading CSV replaces previous list (still 3 rows)
POST /api/admin/clients/{id}/reset-password updates password; client can log in with new password
Role enforcement: client -> /api/admin/* returns 403; admin -> /api/client/* returns 403
GET /api/client/counts returns {purchase_outstanding:3, sales_outstanding:0} after CSV upload
GET /api/client/items?type=purchase returns 3 rows; GET /api/client/items/{id} returns single
Submission validation: mode=no_photo + empty comment -> 400; mode=photo without file -> 400; invalid mode -> 400
Item.status remains 'outstanding' after a failed submission attempt (no state corruption)
GET /api/admin/settings/smtp does not return password; PUT persists; subsequent GET returns configured=true
PUT /api/admin/settings/smtp without password field preserves stored password (configured stays true)
GET /api/admin/submissions returns list; filters (client_id, type) accepted
POST /api/admin/items/{id}/reset sets item back to outstanding

failed.issue: POST /api/admin/clients (and GET /api/admin/clients list, search results, GET /api/client/items, etc.) return MongoDB `_id` field instead of the spec'd `id`. Any consumer following the contract (`response.id`) will get None and subsequent calls to /api/admin/clients/None blow up with `bson.errors.InvalidId: 'None' is not a valid ObjectId` -> 500 Internal Server Error on get/update/delete/upload-csv/reset-password.
failed.evidence: Create client response body: {"_id":"6a418af1ca0d74b12217312d", ...} (no `id` key). Backend logs show repeated 500s on /api/admin/clients/None/*. Item objects in /api/client/items[*] also expose `_id` not `id`.

failed.issue: When SMTP is configured but the host is unreachable (e.g. smtp.example.com), POST /api/client/items/{id}/submit hangs long enough to trigger an upstream 502 Bad Gateway from Cloudflare instead of returning a fast JSON error (503/500). Indicates no SMTP connect timeout / no try-except around send. Could not validate that item.status stays outstanding in this case (gateway returned HTML).
failed.evidence: Request to submit with SMTP host=smtp.example.com returned `502 Bad gateway` HTML page from Cloudflare. Backend has no timeout wrapper around smtplib connect.

failed.issue: Once SMTP is PUT once, there is no admin endpoint to clear/disable it (no DELETE /api/admin/settings/smtp and PUT does not accept a 'disable' flag). This makes it impossible to re-test the 'SMTP not configured -> 503' path after first configuration, and would also block real admins who want to disable email.
failed.evidence: Initial GET on iteration-2 already returned configured=true because state from previous run persisted; no documented way to revert.

failed.issue: Could not be directly verified in this iteration because SMTP was already configured from a prior PUT (see above issue). Logic in server.py should be reviewed to confirm the 503 path actually exists.
failed.evidence: Test attempted but SMTP state was already 'configured=true' before the unconfigured-submit check ran.

action_items: In backend/server.py response models for Client and Item (and any other Mongo-backed object), map `_id` -> `id` (e.g. via Pydantic `alias='_id'` + `populate_by_name=True`, or by manually setting `doc['id'] = str(doc.pop('_id'))` before returning). Apply to: POST/GET/PUT /api/admin/clients, GET /api/client/items, GET /api/client/items/{id}, GET /api/admin/submissions embeddeds.
Wrap SMTP send (smtplib.SMTP/SMTP_SSL) with an explicit connect timeout (e.g. 10s) and try/except that returns HTTP 502/503 with a JSON body; ensure item.status is NOT mutated until send succeeds.
Add an admin endpoint or PUT semantics to disable/clear SMTP config (e.g. DELETE /api/admin/settings/smtp or accept enabled=false) so admins (and tests) can revert state.
Re-run backend tests after fixes — specifically the previously-blocked admin CRUD chain that depended on `id` field.

success_rate.backend: ~80% (19 critical checks pass, 2 HIGH bugs, 2 MEDIUM)
